name: Waialua Farmers Market
tagline: A small but excellent Saturday market inside the historic Waialua Sugar Mill — hyper-local produce and artisan goods.
parking: Free lot at the Sugar Mill
bestTime: Saturday mornings; combine with shopping at the Sugar Mill artisan shops
talkStory: Held inside the historic Waialua Sugar Mill, which closed in 1996 after nearly a century of operation, this small Saturday market channels the agricultural heritage of Waialua. Many vendors are former sugar land lessees who transitioned to diversified farming. Coffee and cacao grown just up the road in Waialua are standout products. Combined with the Sugar Mill's artisan shops -- including Waialua Estate Coffee and Green Gypsy Thrift -- it makes for a perfect North Shore morning.
